# Gulf + Middle East Hybrid Intelligence Skill

Purpose: a vertical specialist skill for AI agents working on Iran sanctions, GCC financial and energy hubs, maritime chokepoint risk (Hormuz, Bab-el-Mandeb, Red Sea), sovereign wealth deployment, and regional geopolitical exposure.

Use this repository when an AI agent needs:
- to reason about Iran sanctions adjacency, OFAC SDN exposure, or US/EU/UK secondary sanctions in a Gulf context;
- to map GCC correspondent banking, trade finance, or sovereign wealth deployment risk;
- to analyse maritime chokepoint disruption (Hormuz, Bab-el-Mandeb, Red Sea) for shipping, energy, or insurance decisions;
- to distinguish Iran-state, IRGC-affiliated, and Iran-private commercial actors with operational precision;
- to produce mechanism-first, role-based, trigger-specific regional risk memos;
- examples, signals, review checklists, and failure-mode guides for Gulf + Middle East risk analysis.

What this repository is not:
- not a factuality verifier, source retriever, or RAG pipeline;
- not legal, compliance, sanctions screening, AML monitoring, vessel screening, or investment advice;
- not an MCP server, CLI tool, validator, schema layer, or eval framework;
- not a replacement for primary OFAC/EU/UK list checks, qualified legal review, or maritime intelligence vendors.

Relationship to the portfolio (three layers, separately maintained):
- Vertical specialist — Gulf + Middle East (this repo): specialist Iran, GCC, maritime-chokepoint risk reasoning.
- Horizontal domain skill: https://github.com/vassiliylakhonin/global-think-tank-analyst — broad strategic-risk memo workflow, memo modes, general policy-risk analysis.
- Vertical specialist — Central Asia Caspian: https://github.com/vassiliylakhonin/central-asia-caspian-hybrid-intelligence-skill — reference when a flow crosses both regions.
- Infrastructure / validation: https://github.com/vassiliylakhonin/Agenda-Intelligence-md — schemas, validation, scoring, evidence audit, CLI / MCP / CI tooling.

Key distinctions this skill enforces:

Iran actor categories (always distinguish where relevant):
- Iran-state: NIOC, CBI of Iran, Iranian government ministries — directly blocked under OFAC comprehensive sanctions.
- IRGC-affiliated: entities operating under or controlled by the IRGC-Quds Force or affiliated networks — SDN-designated; basis for affiliation must be stated (designation, public reporting, network inference).
- Iran-private commercial: Iranian private commercial firms — not categorically blocked; designation status varies; requires entity-level SDN screen.

GCC actor categories (name the specific actor, do not aggregate):
- State actors: government ministries, state-owned enterprises.
- Sovereign wealth: PIF (Saudi), ADIA (Abu Dhabi), Mubadala (Abu Dhabi), QIA (Qatar), KIA (Kuwait).
- Royal-family commercial: entities linked to royal families operating in commercial domains.
- Private commercial: private Gulf firms — risk profile varies; UBO disclosure is the binding variable.

Evidence modes (declare for every output):
- live-source-backed: primary sources retrieved and cited with retrieval date.
- user-provided sources: analysis works from documents the user supplies.
- illustrative source packet: source organizations real, specific values illustrative.
- reasoning-only: no live sources; structural reasoning only; lower confidence.

Core files:
- AGENTS.md — project identity, scope, evidence and safety rules, Definition of Done.
- skills/claude/SKILL.md — Claude variant (Projects setup, web search guidance, extended-context user-provided source workflows).
- skills/codex/SKILL.md — Codex variant (agentic-loop output discipline, JSON output mode, Agenda Intelligence MD pipeline pattern).
- README.md — public documentation.
- docs/source-guide.md — primary sources by domain with re-verification horizons.
- docs/regional-logic.md — scope extension rules, Iran actor distinction, GCC actor distinction.
- docs/risk-archetypes.md — seven recurring risk archetypes.
- examples/README.md — guided route through examples and evidence modes.
- examples/ — worked memo examples in all four evidence modes.
- evals/ — checklist, failure-modes (13 patterns), starter rubric.
- signals/ — bounded, dated signal examples (not official intelligence).
- scripts/validate.py — structural validator for skills, examples, signals, evals and docs.

Agent guidance:
- Always state evidence mode and retrieval date (if live-source-backed) before analysis.
- Distinguish Iran-state, IRGC-affiliated, and Iran-private commercial actors for any Iran-related claim.
- Name specific GCC actors (fund, entity) — do not write "Gulf money" or "Saudi investment" without specificity.
- Do not fabricate: citations, sanctions designations, vessel names, IMO numbers, OFAC actions, AIS data.
- Do not give legal, compliance, AML, sanctions screening, vessel screening, or investment advice.
- Every output must include a limitation note.
- Mechanism before implication: explain how risk transmits (through what channel) before stating what it means.

Forbidden patterns (will be called out in evals):
- "tensions remain elevated" without mechanism.
- "Iran" as an undifferentiated category.
- Specific vessel names, IMO numbers, or OFAC designations without primary-source citation.
- "Monitor closely" without named source and named trigger.
- Any language implying operational compliance clearance or legal advice.

Live signal endpoints:
- Latest signal: https://github.com/vassiliylakhonin/gulf-middle-east-hybrid-intelligence-skill/blob/main/signals/latest.md
- Signal index: https://github.com/vassiliylakhonin/gulf-middle-east-hybrid-intelligence-skill/blob/main/signals/index.json
- JSON Feed: https://github.com/vassiliylakhonin/gulf-middle-east-hybrid-intelligence-skill/blob/main/signals/feed.json
